title: Add tenant scoping to a data-access path
description: Take an unscoped query and bind it to the authenticated tenant, then prove isolation with a negative cross-tenant test.
body: Use when a read or write lacks a tenant predicate. Do not derive the tenant from request input. Actions: resolve the tenant from the authenticated principal; add the tenant predicate to the query at the data layer; centralize it so future queries inherit it; write a negative test that a foreign tenant is denied. Validation signals include the negative test passing and legitimate access unaffected. Risk boundary: an unscoped path is a cross-tenant leak, so treat the fix as high severity, add the data-layer guard, and escalate if other paths share the same gap.
